source_metadata.title_raw: Brett Kordenbrock - A-19-118 - June 26, 2019 - Santa Rosa

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Brett Kordenbrock 
RLALEEDAP 
136 10th St 
Santa Rosa, CA 95402 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-19-118 
Dear Mr. Kordenbrock: 
June 26, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the Act). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict 
of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as 
common law conflict of interest or Section 1090. Also, we are not a finder of fact when rendering 
advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are 
complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should 
change, you should contact us for additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Does the Act prohibit a member of the Design Review Board (the Board) for the City of 
Santa Rosa (the City) from taking part in governmental decisions relating to a proposed project (the 
Project) located 700 feet from the Board member's home where the Project will increase housing 
availability, traffic and general activity in the area? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The Act prohibits the Board member from taking part in decisions about the Project 
because, based on the facts provided, they will have a foreseeable and material effect on the Board 
member's interest in real property. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
You are a member of the Board for the City. The Board will be reviewing the Project that is 
between 500 and 1000 feet from your home. The Project is an adaptive reuse of an old winery 
building that is currently occupied. The current uses of the building include: a restaurant, a 
warehouse, offices, a fitness complex and a vacant lot. Much of the existing building will be reused; 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-19-118 
Page No. 2 
the restaurant and offices will be retained. The Project will create approximately 100 housing units 
for rent, additional commercial and office space. The building will be altered by removing walls 
and adding one or two additional floors. Street frontage will be improved to include more parking 
and a semi-public courtyard/amenity zone. 
The Project is approximately 700 feet from your home. It is not visible from your home, nor 
will it be after the improvements. Your property is separated from the Project by a railroad, a vacant 
lot and a few minor roads. The Project will increase housing availability, traffic and general activity 
in the area. 
ANALYSIS 
Under Section 87100, a conflict of interest exists whenever a public official makes, 
participates in making, or uses his or her position to influence a governmental decision which has a 
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of the official's financial interests. 
A public official must disqualify himself from the governmental decision at issue if the decision 
will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on his interest in real property, 
distinguishable from the public generally. 
Section 87103(b) provides that an official has a financial interest in "[a]ny real property in 
which the public official has a direct or indirect interest of at least $2,000." Section 87103 also 
provides that an official has a financial interest in his or her "personal finances, including those of 
his or her immediate family, also known as the 'personal financial effects' rule."2 Because you own 
your residence worth more than two thousand dollars, you have a real property interest that 
warrants analysis under the Act's conflict of interest provisions. 
A. 
Foreseeability. 
The standard for foreseeability differs depending on whether an interest is explicitly 
involved in the decision. (Regulation 1870L) An interest is explicitly involved in a decision if the 
interest is a named party in, or the subject of, the governmental decision. (Regulation l 8701(an 
Under the facts provided, yout residence is not explicitly involved in the decisions. Thus, "[i]n 
general, if the financial effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical 
or theoretical, [then] it is reasonably foreseeable." (Regulation 18701(b).) 
B. 
Materiality. 
A conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a 
governmental decision on a public official's interest is material. Regulation 18702.2(a) provides a 
list of circumstances under which the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a governmental 
decision on real property in which an official has a financial interest is material. Based on the facts 
provided, the financial effect of the decisions regarding the Project on your real property interest is 
material if the decisions involve property located more than 500 feet but less than 1,000 feet from 
2 Effects on an official's personal finances are not considered separately from the effect on an official's interest 
in real property. (Regulation 18702.S(c).) Thus, we do not consider the personal financial effects rule further. 


File No. A-19-118 
Page No. 3 
the property line of the parcel and would change the parcel's character by substantially altering 
traffic levels, intensity of use or parking. (Regulation 18702.2(a)(8)(D).) 
The facts provided conclusively state that the Project will increase housing availability, 
traffic and general activity in the area. Descriptions of the Project improvements support these 
conclusions, such as the addition of residential housing, commercial and office space, and street 
improvements for more parking. Consequently, decisions regarding the Project involve property 
. 
located 700 feet from the Project and would change the parcel's character by substantially altering 
traffic levels, intensity of use and parking. (Id.) 
F~r the above reasons, you may not make, participate in making, or influence decisions 
regarding the Project. (See Regulation 18707.) 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
JMfjr:aja

sections.question:
Does the Act prohibit a member of the Design Review Board (the Board) for the City of 
Santa Rosa (the City) from taking part in governmental decisions relating to a proposed project (the 
Project) located 700 feet from the Board member's home where the Project will increase housing 
availability, traffic and general activity in the area?
sections.conclusion:
Yes. The Act prohibits the Board member from taking part in decisions about the Project 
because, based on the facts provided, they will have a foreseeable and material effect on the Board 
member's interest in real property.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Act prohibit a member of the Design Review Board (the Board) for the City of 
Santa Rosa (the City) from taking part in governmental decisions relating to a proposed project (the 
Project) located 700 feet from the Board member's home where the Project will increase housing 
availability, traffic and general activity in the area?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The Act prohibits the Board member from taking part in decisions about the Project 
because, based on the facts provided, they will have a foreseeable and material effect on the Board 
member's interest in real property.
